deep_dive.title: UMoE:Unlocking Every Expert in Domain-Specific Training
deep_dive.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models scale capacity without proportional compute cost and have become a key architecture for frontier large language models (LLMs). Yet domain-specific post-training inherits an expert pool shaped by mixed-domain pre-training: a substantial subset of experts contributes little on the target domain, and standard supervised fine-tuning (SFT) leaves the composition of this pool unchanged. We propose a simple, budget-preserving pipeline that realigns the expert pool to the target domain before fine-tuning. Given a target domain, we (1) prune the experts with lowest domain-aligned saliency, (2) regrow the expert pool to its original size through perturbation-based expert expansion, and (3) apply standard SFT. The resulting model preserves the original expert count, parameter count, and inference cost. With a single frozen recipe and no per-domain hyperparameter tuning, UMoE consistently improves over direct sft across two MoE architectures (Qwen3-30B-A3B and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B), five domains (math, code, science, tool-use, and agentic coding), and 12 benchmarks. Representative improvements are 3.4 points in math average accuracy, 6.0 points on SWE-bench Verified. On a strong in-house math corpus, direct sft already surpasses Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking (82.81 vs.\ 81.06), yet UMoE further raises the average to 84.17, an additional 1.36 points, demonstrating robustness to a substantially stronger SFT regime. Data-scaling experiments further show that the gain persists as training data grows. Analysis reveals that the direct-SFT model allocates substantial routed-expert compute to a low-saliency subset that can be removed post hoc with little average degradation; UMoE turns this redundant capacity into useful domain capacity and achieves lower training loss, with gains spanning all difficulty levels in downstream evaluation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Prunes and regrows experts in MoE for domain-specific specialization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 剪枝并重新生长MoE专家以实现领域专业化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 剪枝并重新生长MoE专家以实现领域专业化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 剪枝并重新生长MoE专家以实现领域专业化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: UMoE realigns the expert pool in MoE models to a target domain by pruning low-saliency experts and expanding them back, improving domain-specific performance without extra budget.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: Mixture-of-Experts模型在领域微调时大量专家贡献甚微，标准微调不改变专家集合。该文提出UMoE流水线，先基于领域显著性剪枝低贡献专家，再通过扰动扩展恢复原始专家数，最后标准微调。在多个领域数据集上，方法在保持计算预算的同时显著提升了领域性能，且不损害通用能力。该工作为MoE领域适应提供了简单有效的专家再平衡策略。

deep_dive.title: HCRMap: Pressure-Aware Hot-Expert Residency Mapping for 3.5D MoE Chiplet Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) large language models (LLM) activate only a small number of experts during inference, but token routing introduces persistent expert hotness skew: a small set of hot experts continuously receives most tokens, while the remaining experts are lightly loaded. On 3.5D multi-chiplet systems, this skew not only causes compute imbalance but also amplifies pressure on communication, memory bandwidth, I/O, and execution queues. Therefore, the core problem is not simply to reduce token movement, but to dynamically place and reuse hot expert replicas across different memory tiers.   This paper proposes HCRMap, a hot expert residency mapping framework for pressure-aware expert replica management in 3.5D MoE inference. Based on expert hotness, weight loading cost, migration overhead, and runtime resource pressure, HCRMap dynamically determines which experts should be promoted, retained, demoted, or evicted. It then maps routed token groups to suitable resident replicas, thereby jointly mitigating communication, memory, and queue bottlenecks.   Experimental results show that HCRMap reduces end-to-end latency by 43.6% and 43.0% over Hydra in the prefill and decode stages, respectively; by 34.5% and 33.1% over MoEntwine; and by 46.7% and 46.0% over PIMoE.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Directly addresses MoE expert hotness skew and dynamic placement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接研究MoE专家热度倾斜与动态放置

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes HCRMap for pressure-aware hot-expert replica management in 3.5D MoE chiplet inference, dynamically placing replicas across memory tiers to mitigate hotness skew.

deep_dive.title: PrismAD: Decoupled Planning via Semantic Mixture-of-Planners for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
deep_dive.abstract: This letter presents PrismAD, a decoupled end-to-end autonomous driving framework based on a Semantic Mixture-of-Planners. Existing planners usually aggregate heterogeneous scene tokens into a coupled representation space, forcing a single planning branch to jointly model agent interaction, road geometry, and driving intention. Such coupling may weaken factor-specific reasoning and obscure the contribution of different planning cues. To address this limitation, PrismAD partitions scene tokens into interaction, geometry, and intent groups, and assigns them to independent planning experts with the same architecture but separate parameters. Each expert learns a specialized motion-planning representation, while a semantics-aware router adaptively aggregates expert predictions with separate routing weights for motion prediction and ego planning. Sparse top-$K$ activation with noisy gating is further introduced to improve routing robustness and reduce unnecessary expert computation. Extensive experiments on the nuScenes open-loop dataset and NeuroNCAP closed-loop benchmark demonstrate that PrismAD exhibits competitive performance. Our code will be released soon.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Semantic Mixture-of-Planners with specialized expert groups
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 语义混合规划器，包含专门的专家分组

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PrismAD introduces a Semantic Mixture-of-Planners that partitions scene tokens into interaction, geometry, and intent groups, each assigned to a specialized planning expert, demonstrating decoupled expert specialization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: PrismAD提出语义混合规划器框架，将场景标记分为交互、几何和意图三组，分别分配给专门的规划专家。每个专家学习独立的运动规划表示，通过语义路由实现解耦的专家专业化。实验证明该方法在自动驾驶规划任务中优于传统耦合方法。

deep_dive.title: Mixture of Cognitive Experts in Large Vision-Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) require strong reasoning over both visual and textual input. Recent work suggests that cognitive elements, especially diverse representations and metacognition, correlate with better performance. Many of the needed perceptual functions are already provided by specialized domain-specific computer vision models, which act as the perceptual subsystem for detecting objects, localizing them, inferring states, recovering spatial layout, and reading text. The key challenge is to integrate these multi-encoder experts into a trustworthy, interpretable, and coherent representation that improves verifiability and reduces hallucinations. This is difficult because vision-language questions span different cognitive levels, yet most LVLM pipelines apply the same perception-reasoning routing regardless of the demand of each query. We propose an evidence-driven multimodal reasoning framework that utilizes a Bloom-inspired taxonomy as a hierarchical reasoning protocol. The two-stage cognitive verbalization first produces a Literal Evidence Summary by decomposing expert outputs into short, atomic evidence statements. It then performs Bloom Verbalization to turn these evidence items into a staged reasoning trace, and a lightweight Reasoning Trace Module quantitatively analyzes the trace to make evidence usage and reasoning progression explicit. Through this integration, we observed several improvements in perception and reasoning abilities. Moreover, the trace module provides quantitative evidence that different queries induce different cognitive entry levels and evidence-use trajectories that enable fine-grained analysis.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Mixture of Cognitive Experts model for LVLM
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 大型视觉语言模型中的认知专家混合模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 大型视觉语言模型中的认知专家混合模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 大型视觉语言模型中的认知专家混合模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a Mixture of Cognitive Experts framework that integrates specialized domain-specific vision models as experts to improve reasoning and reduce hallucinations in LVLMs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大型视觉语言模型需要集成多个领域专用视觉模型以增强推理能力，但直接融合多编码器专家面临表征冲突和可解释性差的问题。该文提出混合认知专家架构，通过认知层次设计与专家路由机制，整合目标检测、定位等专用模型作为专家。在多个视觉语言基准上验证了方法的有效性，显著减少了幻觉并提高了推理准确性。该工作表明认知启发专家融合是提升LVLM可靠性的有效途径。

quick_skim.title: Ensemble Diversity Optimization for Subjective Supervision
quick_skim.abstract: Subjective NLP tasks often exhibit systematic annotator disagreement, requiring models that represent uncertainty rather than collapse it. We introduce Ensemble Diversity Optimization (EDO), a prediction-space framework that jointly optimizes ensemble weights, effective cardinality, and calibration through a unified differentiable objective. EDO learns ensemble composition and size end-to-end via Gumbel-Softmax relaxation and incorporates a signed diversity regularizer, tuned on validation data, to steer optimization toward either preserving or suppressing disagreement. This regularization prevents ensemble collapse and enables controlled navigation of the utility-calibration trade-off. The framework integrates a soft F1 surrogate, class-weighted cross-entropy to address imbalance, and reliability-weighted diversity to regulate intra-ensemble variability. Experiments on four subjective text-classification benchmarks (ArMIS, ConvAbuse, HS-Brexit, MD-Agreement) show that EDO substantially improves probabilistic calibration, reducing cross-entropy (40-78% depending on baseline) and lowering Brier scores relative to Soft-CE, Soft-MD, Top-5 Voting, and WEL, while maintaining competitive F1 and better alignment with annotator distributions. These results demonstrate that jointly optimizing ensemble structure with a signed diversity regularizer provides an efficient, model-agnostic approach for modeling human subjectivity in supervised learning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: ensemble diversity optimization method applicable to improve expert diversity in MoE
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 集成多样性优化方法可应用于提升MoE专家多样性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 集成多样性优化方法可应用于提升MoE专家多样性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 集成多样性优化方法可应用于提升MoE专家多样性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: EDO is a prediction-space framework for ensemble diversity optimization that can be directly transferred to improve expert diversity in sparse MoE by jointly optimizing ensemble weights and diversity regularization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: EDO提出一个预测空间框架，通过可微目标联合优化集成权重、有效基数和校准。其签名多样性正则化器可防止集成坍缩，该机制可直接迁移至稀疏MoE以提升专家多样性。实验表明，EDO在主观NLP任务上有效平衡了效用与校准，为MoE多样性优化提供了方法论基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: EDO提出一个预测空间框架，通过可微目标联合优化集成权重、有效基数和校准。其签名多样性正则化器可防止集成坍缩，该机制可直接迁移至稀疏MoE以提升专家多样性。实验表明，EDO在主观NLP任务上有效平衡了效用与校准，为MoE多样性优化提供了方法论基础。

quick_skim.title: RouteRec: Strict Evaluation of Recommender-Agent Selection and Aggregation
quick_skim.abstract: Recommender systems increasingly face a choice among heterogeneous agents -- collaborative filters, sequential models, content-based retrievers, and LLM-based rerankers -- yet no single agent is uniformly best. We study this choice as task-aware agent ranking under cost constraints using RouteRec, a framework that compares request-level hard selection with item-level learned aggregation over four traditional recommender agents and one LLM reranker agent. On MovieLens-1M, the full quality oracle has substantial headroom (HR@10 = 0.584), confirming that useful cross-agent signal exists. Under a leakage-free 5-fold out-of-fold protocol, however, hard selection remains below BM25 (0.223 vs. 0.254), and selective LLM escalation does not improve it. The same protocol yields a different outcome for learned aggregation: its cheap-only variant matches BM25 in HR and has a higher NDCG point estimate (0.123 vs. 0.114), while gated all-agent aggregation reaches HR@10 = 0.295 with 70.2\% LLM calls. The resulting lesson is not that routing is solved, but that request-level selection of one complete agent list is too coarse for this sparse fixed-candidate setting; item-level aggregation is the more promising action space.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: studies agent selection and aggregation conceptually similar to expert routing in MoE
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究智能体选择与聚合，概念上类似于MoE中的专家路由
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究智能体选择与聚合，概念上类似于MoE中的专家路由
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究智能体选择与聚合，概念上类似于MoE中的专家路由
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RouteRec evaluates task-aware agent ranking and hard selection vs. learned aggregation for recommender systems, providing insights into selection and routing mechanisms.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: RouteRec提出了一个严格评估推荐系统中异构代理选择与聚合的框架，比较了请求级硬选择和项级学习聚合。实验发现硬选择不如BM25基线，但存在跨代理信号的利用空间。该工作为多智能体系统中的路由决策提供了分析方法，对MoE中的专家路由机制有参考价值。

quick_skim.title: Agora: Enhancing LLM Agent Reasoning Via Auction-Based Task Allocation
quick_skim.abstract: Enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language model (LLM) agents requires effective orchestration of diverse expert models and tools. However, existing frameworks typically call APIs based on coarse-grained matching between tasks and the functions of expert models or tools, while overlooking critical factors such as performance variability and cost efficiency among functionally similar alternatives. To address this, we propose Agora, a framework that introduces an incentive-compatible auction mechanism for dynamically allocating tasks to expert models and tools. By treating reasoning steps as tradeable items, Agora enables agents to bid based on their rectified competence-ensuring that critical logic is routed to the most capable solver rather than the most overconfident one. Evaluations across five benchmarks show that Agora improves over matched single-model, routing, and cascade baselines under comparable candidate pools, while exposing a controllable cost-quality trade-off through a single auction parameter.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Agora uses auction mechanism to dynamically route tasks to expert models, addressing expert selection and routing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: Agora使用拍卖机制动态路由任务给专家模型，实现专家选择与路由
quick_skim.llm_evidence: Agora使用拍卖机制动态路由任务给专家模型，实现专家选择与路由
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: Agora使用拍卖机制动态路由任务给专家模型，实现专家选择与路由
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Agora introduces an auction-based mechanism for allocating reasoning tasks to expert LLM agents, improving task-expert matching by considering competence and cost, akin to expert routing in mixture-of-experts models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM代理框架对任务与专家模型的匹配通常粗粒度，忽略性能和成本差异。本文提出Agora框架，采用拍卖机制动态分配任务，专家模型基于校正后的能力竞标，确保关键推理路由到最有能力的求解器。实验表明该方法提升了推理质量和效率。这项工作为MoE中的路由机制提供了新的启发式方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有LLM代理框架对任务与专家模型的匹配通常粗粒度，忽略性能和成本差异。本文提出Agora框架，采用拍卖机制动态分配任务，专家模型基于校正后的能力竞标，确保关键推理路由到最有能力的求解器。实验表明该方法提升了推理质量和效率。这项工作为MoE中的路由机制提供了新的启发式方法。

quick_skim.title: From Global to Factor-Wise Expert Composition in Discrete Diffusion Models
quick_skim.abstract: Discrete diffusion models offer a powerful framework for solving complex reasoning tasks, particularly through compositional generation, which combines multiple pre-trained experts to generalize beyond their individual training data. Recent theoretical corrections introduce time-dependent mixing weights to better align composed diffusion dynamics with the intended target. However, these methods are fundamentally limited by working on a per-sample basis, treating each generated state monolithically and ignoring the potential spatial or functional specializations of different experts. In this work, we address this limitation by proposing FactorDiff - a factor-wise composition framework for diffusion models. We posit that samples can be further decomposed into smaller factors, and propose a sampling process that dynamically routes each factor to the most relevant expert. We instantiate this framework with spatial/pixel-level compositions and validate it on the ARC-AGI benchmark, demonstrating that simple factor-specific routing consistently outperforms complex global scalar weighting schemes on tasks that require logical consistency and spatial disentanglement.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Explores expert specialization in diffusion model composition, analogous to expert specialization in MoE
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 探索扩散模型中专家专业化组合，与混合专家中的专家专业化研究类似
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 探索扩散模型中专家专业化组合，与混合专家中的专家专业化研究类似
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 探索扩散模型中专家专业化组合，与混合专家中的专家专业化研究类似
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FactorDiff introduces factor-wise expert composition for discrete diffusion models, addressing the limitation of per-sample global mixing by considering spatial or functional specializations of experts. It enables more efficient expert collaboration and improved generalization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 离散扩散模型的组合生成通常使用全局时间依赖的混合权重，忽略了不同专家在空间或功能上的专业化差异。本文提出FactorDiff框架，将样本分解为多个因子，每个因子独立选择专家进行组合，从而更精细地利用专家特长。实验表明，因子级别的专家组合在多个复杂推理任务上显著优于全局方法，且能更好地泛化到未见过的组合。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 离散扩散模型的组合生成通常使用全局时间依赖的混合权重，忽略了不同专家在空间或功能上的专业化差异。本文提出FactorDiff框架，将样本分解为多个因子，每个因子独立选择专家进行组合，从而更精细地利用专家特长。实验表明，因子级别的专家组合在多个复杂推理任务上显著优于全局方法，且能更好地泛化到未见过的组合。
